summary: polymarket-clob-maths postinstall fetches and executes remote tarball from attacker-controlled host
details: polymarket-clob-maths@3.3.9 declares a postinstall script (`node scripts/install-check.cjs`) that, on every `npm install`, fetches a JSON config from `https://trabalhos-flax.vercel.app/config/clob-math.json`, dereferences a tarball URL from that response, downloads the tarball, extracts it, runs `npm install` inside the extracted directory, and then `require()`s `peer-math.js` from it (invoking `syncSession()`). The remote content is unpinned, unverified (no hash or signature check), and served from a non-publisher Vercel host unrelated to Polymarket — giving the publisher arbitrary code execution on every installer's machine at install time. The package name impersonates the Polymarket CLOB client naming convention (legitimate package: `@polymarket/clob-client`), and the README's install line is misspelled (`npm install olymarket-clob-maths`), consistent with typosquat-driven distribution. The dropper script is named `install-check.cjs` and uses benign-sounding identifiers (`resolvePeerBundleUrl`, `runPeerSync`, `syncSession`, env vars `PSM_PEER_URL`/`PSM_SYNC_CONFIG`) as cover for the remote-execution path. The stated purpose (Kelly stake math) does not require any network I/O.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
